title: 第 70 節
en: 3. By repeating the first verse three times, they 1 Because Vira^-, a foot of ten syllables, is food. Rv. X, 120, 1 a= . . 10 Rv. VIII, 69, 2 a= 7 Syllable pu = . . 1 IK 3 Seven in the head and two in the body ; sapta vai sirsha«ya/£ prawa dvav avaii^av iti.
5 Each pada has seven syllables, the third only six; but a seventh syllable is gained by pronouncing the y as i. Comm.
6 Because it has four padas.

I ARAiVYAKA, 3 ADHYAYA, 8 KHAA^A, 8. 1 85 become twenty-five. The trunk is the twenty-fifth, and Pra^apati is the twenty-fifth. There are ten fingers on his hands, ten toes on his feet, two legs, two arms, and the trunk the twenty-fifth. He adorns that trunk as the twenty-fifth. Now this day consists of twenty-five, and the Stoma hymn of that day consists of twenty-five : it becomes the same through the same. Therefore the two, the day and the hymn, are twenty-five. This is the twenty-fifth with regard to the body.

4. Next, with regard to the deities : The eye, the ear, the mind, speech, and breath, these five deities (powers) have entered into that person (purusha), and that person entered into the five deities. He is wholly pervaded there with his limbs to the very hairs and nails. Therefore all beings to the very insects are born as pervaded (by the deities or senses) 1.
5. This has been declared by a i?zshi (Rv. X, 6. 'A thousandfold are these fifteen hymns ;' — for five arise from ten 2.
7. ' As large as heaven and earth, so large is it ;' — verily, the self (^ivatman) is as large as heaven and earth.
8. 'A thousandfold are the thousand powers3;' —
1 The commentator takes this in a different sense, explaining atra, there, as the body pervaded by the person, yet afterwards stating that all beings are born, pervaded by the senses.

2 The commentator explains uktha, hymns, as members or organs. They are the five, and they spring from the ten, i.e. from the five elements (earth, water, fire, wind, and ether), forming part of the father and mother each, and therefore called ten, or a decade. Da^ata^ is explained by bhutadajakat.
3 The application of the senses to a thousand different objects.
t86 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
by saying this the poet pleases the hymns (the senses), and magnifies them.
9. ' As far as Brahman reaches, so far reaches speech ; ' — wherever there is Brahman, there is a word ; and wherever there is a word, there is Brahman, this was intended.
10. The first of the hymns among all those hymns has nine verses. Verily, there are nine pra;/as (openings), and it serves for their benefit.
11. Then follows a hymn of six verses. Verily, the seasons are six, and it serves to obtain them.
12. Then follows a hymn of five verses. Verily, the Paiikti consists of five feet. Verily, Paiikti is food, and it serves for the gaining of proper food.
13. Then follows a tristich. Three are these threefold worlds, and it serves to conquer them.

14. These verses become Brzhatis1, that metre being immortal, leading to the world of the Devas. That body of verses is the trunk (of the bird represented by the whole i"astra), and thus it is. He who knows this comes by this way (by making the verses the trunk of the bird) near to the immortal Self, yea, to the immortal Self2.

1 Each foot of the Trish/ubh has eleven syllables, to which seven are added from the Nada hymn. This gives eighteen syllables for each pada. Two padas therefore give thirty-six syllables, and this is a Br/hati. In this manner the twenty-three verses of the hymns yield forty- six FWhatis. Comm.
2 He obtains a birth among the gods by means of this Mahavrata ceremonial, if performed with meditation and a right understanding of its hidden meaning.
I ARA2VYAKA, \ ADHYAYA, I KHAiVDA, \. 1 87 FOURTH ADHYAYA.
First Khanda.
1. Next comes the Sudadohas 1 verse. Sudadohas is breath, and thereby he joins all joints with breath.
2. Next follow the neck verses. They recite them as Ushwih, according to their metre 2.
3. Next comes (again) the Sudadohas verse. Sudadohas is breath, and thereby he joins all joints with breath.

4. Next follows the head. That is in Gayatri verses. The Gayatri is the beginning of all metres 3; the head the first of all members. It is in Arkavat verses (Rv. I, 7, 1-9) 4. Arka is Agni. They are nine verses. The head consists of nine pieces. He recites the tenth verse, and that is the skin and the hairs on the head. It serves for reciting one verse more than (the nine verses contained in) the Stoma5.

1 The Nishkevalya-jastra is represented in the shape of a bird, consisting of trunk, neck, head, vertebrae, wings, tail, and stomach. Before describing the hymns which form the neck, another hymn has to be mentioned, called Sudadohas, which has to be recited at the end of the hymns, described before, which form the trunk. Sudadohas is explained as ' yielding milk,' and because that word occurs in the verse, the verse is called Sudadohas. It follows on the Nada verse, Rv. VIII, 69, 3. Cf. Ait. Ar. I, 5, 1, 7.
2 They occur in another jakha, and are to be recited such as they are, without any insertions. They are given by £aunaka, Ait. Ar. V, 2, 1.
zh: 3. 藉由重複第一行詩句三次，他們¹ 因為毗羅（Vira-）乃十音節之足，是食物。梨俱吠陀 X, 120, 1a=...10 梨俱吠陀 VIII, 69, 2a=7 音節 pu=...1 印度經典 3 七在頭部，二在身體；sapta vai sirsha«ya/£ praw dvav avaii^av iti。
每一個音節段（pada）共有七個音節，第三個除外，只有六個；但透過將y讀作i，可得到第七個音節。注疏。
因為它具有四個 pada（韻腳）。

《阿蘭尼亞卡》（Āranyaka）第三章（Adhyaya）第八節（Khaa）第八段 變成二十五。軀幹是第二十五，而梵天（Prajāpati）也是第二十五。他的雙手上有十根手指，雙腳上有十根腳趾，兩條腿，兩隻手臂，以及軀幹第二十五。他將那軀幹作為第二十五而加以裝飾。現在這一天由二十五組成，而那一天的讚美詩（Stoma）也由二十五組成：透過相同之物而變成相同。因此這兩者——這一天與讚美詩——都是二十五。這就身體而言的第二十五。
4. 其次，關於諸提婆：眼睛、耳朵、心智、言語與呼吸，此五提婆（力量）已進入該人（purusha），而該人亦進入此五提婆。他以其四肢乃至毛髮和指甲完全被這五提婆所滲透。因此一切生物，乃至昆蟲，皆生而被滲透（由諸提婆或感官）¹。
5. 這一點已由一位 rishi（梨俱吠陀第十卷第六章）申明：「這十五首讚歌有千般；」——蓋五出於十。
7. 「如天地般廣大，它也就如此廣大；」——誠然，自我（ātman）如天地般廣大。
8. 「千倍有千種力量3」——
註釋者對此採取了不同的解釋，將 atra（此處）解釋為被人格所遍透的身體，然而其後又述及所有眾生乃是被感官所遍透而誕生。
評論家將 uktha（讚歌）解釋為身體的肢體或器官。它們是五個，並由十個衍生而來，即由五大元素（土、水、火、風和以太）衍生，各自構成父親和母親的一部分，因此稱為十或十進制。Da^ata^ 由 bhutadajakat 解釋。
3 將感官應用於千般不同的對象。
吠陀層（梵：aitareya-āraṇyaka）
詩人藉由這樣說話來取悅於讚美詩（感官），並誇大它們。
9. 「梵天所及之處，言語亦所及之處；」——凡有梵天（Brahman）之處，即有言語；凡有言語之處，即有梵天，此乃其本旨。
10. 在所有讚歌中，最初的讚歌有九節經文。誠然，有九個 *pra;/as*（開篇），它乃為其利益而作。
11. 其後繼以六詩節之讚歌。誠然，季節有六，故此讚歌乃用以獲得諸季節者。
12. 隨後是一首五詩節的讚歌。確實，帕伊克蒂（Paiikti）由五個足構成。確實，帕伊克蒂是食物，它用於獲得適當的食物。
13. 其次是三行頌文。世間有此三重世界，誦此三行頌文可征服它們。
14. 這些詩句成為婆利哈蒂韻律（Brzhatis），該韻律乃不朽之物，引向提婆之世界。這些詩句之體乃是（整部夏斯特拉所代表之鳥的）軀幹，事實即是如此。凡知此者得由此道（藉由使詩句成為鳥的軀幹）趨近於不朽的自我，誠然，趨近於不朽的自我。
特里什/烏布（Trish/ubh）的每一足有十一個音節，再加上納達讚歌（Nada hymn）的七個音節，每一足就有十八個音節。因此兩足便產生三十六個音節，這就是一個布里哈提（Brihati）。按此方式，讚歌的二十三個詩句就產生四十六個布里哈提。——註疏家
他若以冥思與對其隱密義理的正確理解來執行此摩訶誓願（Mahavrata）儀式，便能獲得在諸提婆中的一次誕生。
第四章 第一節 第一品 第187頁
第一章
接著是蘇達多哈斯（Sudadohas）詩句。蘇達多哈斯是呼吸，藉此他以呼吸連接所有的關節。
2. 接著是頸部聖句。他們按照其韻律將其誦讀為烏什維赫（Ushwih）²。
3. 接著（再次）是修陀多哈斯（Sudadohas）詩句。修陀多哈斯是呼吸，由此他以呼吸連結所有的關節。
4. 接著是頭部。這在《蓋亞特里》(Gayatri) 詩句中。蓋亞特里是所有詩格的開端³；頭部是所有身體部位中首先的。這在《阿卡瓦特》(Arkavat) 詩句中 (梨俱吠陀 I, 7, 1-9)⁴。阿卡 (Arka) 是阿格尼 (Agni)。共有九首詩句。頭部由九個部分組成。他誦唱第十首詩句，那代表頭部的皮膚和頭髮。這用於誦唱比（《贊歌》(Stoma) 中所包含的九首詩句）多出一首的詩句⁵。

尼什克瓦利亞經（Nishkevalya-jastra）以鳥的形狀來呈現，由軀幹、頸部、頭部、脊椎骨、翅膀、尾部及胃部組成。在描述構成頸部的讚歌之前，必須先提到另一首讚歌，稱為蘇達多哈斯（Sudadohas），它應在前面描述構成軀幹的讚歌之後誦讀。蘇達多哈斯被解釋為「產出乳汁」（yielding milk），正因為該詞出現在該詩句中，故該詩句稱為蘇達多哈斯。它接在納達詩句之後，見梨俱吠陀VIII, 69, 3。參考艾達瑞亞阿蘭亞卡I, 5, 1, 7。
他們出現在另一部夜柔（jakha）中，應當按原文背誦，不加任何增補。這些內容由瑜那卡（Çaunaka）所傳述，見《愛達羅耶書》（Ait. Ar.）第五卷第二篇第一章。
